texto: Do what you have to do when you have to do it, so that you can do what you want to do when you want to do it.
explicacao: Orientação direta de M.K. sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “Do what you have to do when you have to do it, so that you can do what you want to do when you wa…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, inspirational-life, útil em momentos de inspiracao e reflexao.

texto: who are youreally?you are not a nameor a height, or a weightor a genderyou are not an ageand you are not where you are fromyou are your favorite booksand the songs stuck in your headyou are your thoughts and what you eat for breakfaston Saturday morningsyou are a thousand thingsbut everyone choosesto see the million thingsyou are notyou are not where you are fromyou arewhere you are goingand i'd liketo go theretoo
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de m.k que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “who are youreally?you are not a nameor a height, or a weightor a genderyou are not an ageand you …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, identity, who-you-are, com foco em bom-dia e termino.
